summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the image processing endpoint can fetch arbitrary external URLs without validation, demonstrating SSRF vulnerability that could expose internal network resources.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that the server fetches attacker-controlled URLs and returns their content, proving SSRF capability to access internal services or cloud metadata.
actual_observed_result: The server successfully fetched httpbin.org URLs through the /images endpoint, returning the content of external resources without any URL validation or whitelisting.
verification_method: The reproduction script uses curl to request the /images endpoint with an external URL parameter. It verifies SSRF by checking if the response contains content from the attacker-controlled URL (httpbin.org). Success is confirmed when UUID data or HTTP 200 status is returned from the fetched resource.
evidence_produced_by_run: Server successfully fetched https://httpbin.org/uuid and returned UUID content
Server processed request for http://httpbin.org/status/200 with HTTP 200 response
No URL validation errors were logged, confirming the endpoint accepts any http/https URL
The vulnerable code path imageProcessor.ts:204 fetch() was executed without restrictions

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the latest commit, and builds the Docker image from that checkout
